versionNotes: Sprint 4 W13 Look-back 1 (5 Whys) — extra-credit meta-reflection form activity with optional AI sparring partner on sections 2-5
title: Look-back 1: 5 Whys
description: Open your Sprint 1 5 Whys assignment and read what you wrote. This look-back isn't about redoing it. It's about looking at the assignment itself — what it was trying to develop in you, whether you let it, and what that tells you about how you engage with hard work.

questions.infoBlock:
Step 1 of 6 · Open the original

Open your Sprint 1 5 Whys assignment in a new tab and keep it open while you work through this look-back.

🔗 Open Sprint 1: 5 Whys Analysis →

Opens in a new tab so you can read it alongside this page.
questions.prompt: Open the link above to your Sprint 1 5 Whys assignment. Read what you wrote. Don't try to fix it. Just read it. Then come back here.

questions.sectionTitle: What was this assignment trying to develop in you?
questions.infoBlock:
Step 2 of 6 · The hidden intent

What was this assignment trying to develop in you?

Not what the prompt literally said — what was the actual capability the assignment was trying to build? Name it in your own words.
questions.prompt: Not what the prompt literally said — what was the actual capability the assignment was trying to build? Name it in your own words.
questions.placeholder: Example: It wasn't really about getting to "the" root cause. It was about whether I'd keep asking when the answer started getting uncomfortable.

questions.sectionTitle: What would a genuine attempt at this assignment have looked like for you specifically?
questions.infoBlock:
Step 3 of 6 · A genuine attempt

What would a genuine attempt at this assignment have looked like for you specifically?

What would you have had to do, think, or face for this assignment to actually work on you? Be specific to your situation, not generic.
questions.prompt: What would you have had to do, think, or face for this assignment to actually work on you? Be specific to your situation, not generic.
questions.placeholder: Example: I would've had to admit my "root cause" was really about my dad's expectations, not my study habits — and I wasn't ready to write that down where a professor could read it.

questions.sectionTitle: What would the wrong way to approach this assignment have been — and did you do it that way?
questions.infoBlock:
Step 4 of 6 · The wrong way

What would the wrong way to approach this assignment have been — and did you do it that way?

There's always a wrong way: rushing, picking the comfortable answer, performing for the grade, copying a structure without thinking. Name the wrong way for THIS assignment. Then say honestly whether you took it.
questions.prompt: There's always a wrong way: rushing, picking the comfortable answer, performing for the grade, copying a structure without thinking. Name the wrong way for THIS assignment. Then say honestly whether you took it.
questions.placeholder: Example: The wrong way was to write five whys that each sounded reasonable but stayed above the waterline. I did it that way. I can tell because my final "why" is basically a study-skills tip, not a truth about me.

questions.sectionTitle: What did you actually gain (or would have gained) from this assignment?
questions.infoBlock:
Step 5 of 6 · What you gained

What did you actually gain (or would have gained) from this assignment?

If you engaged with it genuinely, what did you walk away with? If you didn't, what would you have walked away with if you had?
questions.prompt: If you engaged with it genuinely, what did you walk away with? If you didn't, what would you have walked away with if you had?
questions.placeholder: Example: If I'd actually pushed through I'd have a usable warning signal — when my "why" starts sounding reasonable too fast, that's exactly the moment I'm lying to myself. That would've been worth the discomfort.

questions.sectionTitle: What does this look-back tell you?
questions.infoBlock:
Step 6 of 6 · Closing reflection

What does this look-back reveal about how you handle hard truths about yourself?

Short answer. One or two sentences. No AI help on this one — it's yours.
questions.prompt: In one or two sentences: what does this look-back reveal about how you handle hard truths about yourself?
